title: Protease Activity Assay by Skim Milk Agar Method
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"30df5","text":"This protocol describes the detection of protease activity in 'Biocontrol bacteria' using a skim milk agar plate assay adapted from Khalil et al. (2014). Casein hydrolysis is visualized as clear zones around bacterial colonies, providing a qualitative measure of proteolytic activity.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dsfrl","text":"Prepare all reagents fresh where indicated, particularly FeCl₃ and culture media.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"81fb2","text":"Maintain aseptic technique throughout to prevent contamination.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"n772","text":"Include both positive and negative controls for reliable interpretation of enzymatic activity.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d85k2","text":"Perform each assay in replicates and repeat independently to ensure reproducibility.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"2ksfu","text":"Sterilize all glassware and media in advance.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"58b2s","text":"Calibrate pipettes and spectrophotometer if measuring OD₆₀₀ for inoculum preparation.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1p8rs","text":"Ensure incubator is set to 28 °C and functioning properly.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"846d5","text":"Label all plates and controls before inoculation to avoid mix-ups.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"6j30q","text":"Handle microbial cultures under BSL-2 practices to prevent accidental exposure or cross-contamination.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f0tpb","text":"Autoclave all biological waste (plates, cultures) before disposal.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c3605","text":"Wear gloves, lab coat, and eye protection when preparing and handling chemicals (FeCl₃, glutaraldehyde, Congo red, etc.).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5m4gb","text":"Avoid opening plates unnecessarily during incubation to reduce contamination risk.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"dufna","text":"-       Skim Milk Powder","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b37ja","text":"-       Agar","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dqsdb","text":"-       Weighboat","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"40rha","text":"-       Scale","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dl2d0","text":"-       250mL Graduated Cylinder","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ao522","text":"-       2 500mL Beakers","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8s1nn","text":"-       2 Stir Bars ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eukd8","text":"-       Magnetic Stir Rod","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"pb2o","text":"-       Stir Plate","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5v7jq","text":"-       Deionized Water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Dissolve 25 g skim milk powder in 200 mL deionized water with stirring. Dissolve 5 g agar in 200 mL deionized water separately. Combine the two solutions in a 1 L flask, rinse beakers with small volumes of water, and adjust the final volume to 500 mL . Mix thoroughly and sterilize at 121 °C for 15 min . Cool to ~50 °C, pour into sterile Petri dishes, and allow to solidify.

Prepare bacterial suspensions of JDE115 as described previously. Spot 10 µL of each dilution onto sectors of the skim milk agar plates. Apply LB broth as negative control. Apply undiluted Pseudomonas sp. as positive control.
Incubate plates at 28 °C for 24 h . Assess protease activity by measuring clear hydrolysis zones around colonies. Record zone diameter (colony + halo vs colony alone).
creator: Md Sahadat Ali
